title: Indexes, Query Plans, Operator Costs, and Responsible Tuning · Result-Grain Case: Explain Plans
summary: A growing ticket dataset makes queue and analytics queries slow, but each new index adds write and storage cost. Planned artifact: Add and verify the sql explain plans increment in an evidence-backed query performance dossier. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Read EXPLAIN evidence while separating estimates, actual measurements, and engine-specific plan vocabulary.
Estimate selectivity and compare scans, seeks, lookups, and join access paths from data distribution.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.sql:
-- sql-basics cumulative relational data portfolio.
-- Each increment declares grain, predicts cardinality or changed rows, runs in fresh browser SQLite,
-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.

SELECT sqlite_version() AS browser_sqlite_version;

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Declare result or mutation grain, keys, NULL meaning, and transaction boundary; predict cardinality and invariants; run the smallest query; reconcile counts and plans; then test changed data and dialect limits.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Queue queries filter status and severity, order by opened_at, and join owner_id across 4000 modeled rows. Compare baseline and indexed plans, preserve results, and account for insert and storage cost. A defensible solution applies Explain Plans, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to an evidence-backed query performance dossier.

content.code:
EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN
SELECT ticket_id, owner_id, opened_at
FROM tickets
WHERE status = 'open' AND severity IN ('high', 'critical')
ORDER BY opened_at, ticket_id;
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_tickets_queue
  ON tickets(status, severity, opened_at, ticket_id);
content.caption: Runnable relational query or data-system contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

steps.title: Build Explain Plans evidence · Theory Explain Plans
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable SQLite increment headed "-- Evidence: sql-explain-plans-u7mlzi" with a concrete grain, hypothesis, or prediction; index, access-path, plan, or controlled-performance evidence; and an observation, reconciliation, or verification tied to returned rows, changed rows, an error, or a plan.

Evidence target: Build Explain Plans evidence · Theory Explain Plans.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Explain Plans into observable performance evidence. For Build Explain Plans evidence · Theory Explain Plans, keep Explain Plans connected to executable relational evidence.

content.code:
-- Evidence: sql-explain-plans-u7mlzi
-- grain: declare the exact row or mutation grain before running
EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN
SELECT ticket_id, opened_at FROM tickets
WHERE status = 'open' AND severity = 'high'
ORDER BY opened_at, ticket_id;
-- observation: reconcile the returned rows, changed count, error, or plan with the prediction
